title: Steve McQueen on ‘Widows’ and Reinventing the Heist Movie | Interview (Ep. 100)
format: Sean Fennessey talks with Steve McQueen about turning a 1983 British miniseries into a Chicago heist movie about four women, and why he thinks the genre had no rules to begin with.

films.title: Widows
films.note: McQueen saw the Lynda La Plante TV series at 13 and identified with the widows; he and Gillian Flynn moved the story to Chicago to fold in race, politics, and gender.

films.title: Ocean's Eleven
films.note: The heist template McQueen is deliberately not making: he wanted the domestic stuff, like childcare and dogs, that heist movies usually skip.

films.title: Some Like It Hot
films.note: His answer to the last-great-thing-you've-seen question. He rewatched it and marveled at how fast Billy Wilder gets moving: a train to Florida inside ten minutes.

referenced.group: McQueen's filmography, cited in the intro

TV: Widows (the 1983 Lynda La Plante ITV miniseries Widows is adapted from)
Cross-promo: Villains (new Ringer podcast promoted in the cold open)
